description:
Intermittent failure in the full parallel test suite (pre-commit hook), observed 2026-06-06:

  thread 'tracker::prime::tests::the_committed_pointer_overrides_the_wire_remote' panicked at src/tracker/prime.rs:117:9:
  assertion left == right failed
    left:  d92ef1b... (got)
   right:  8c237d5... (expected committed-pointer SHA)

PASSES in isolation (cargo test <name> --test-threads=1) and on commit retry — so it is a parallel-execution race, not a logic bug. Two SHAs diverging points at shared mutable git/temp state or an env race between sibling tests (cf. the known global-env-race pattern: HOME bl-bfa8, PATH bl-ad4b, both fixed by isolating the env/dir). Likely a tracker-prime test writing/reading a pointer in a dir another test mutates concurrently, or relying on process-wide CWD/env.

Repro: run the full suite repeatedly (the pre-commit hook) until it trips. Fix by isolating the test's repo/temp/env (per-test tempdir + no process-global env mutation), as the prior env-race fixes did.

Surfaced while folding the observability topic (bl-b58a, doc-only) — the flake blocked the unrelated commit until retry.
title: Flaky test under parallel run: tracker::prime::the_committed_pointer_overrides_the_wire_remote
